name: azure-storage-file-share-py
description: Manage Azure Storage file shares using Python SDK for cloud-native and lift-and-shift scenarios.
capabilities: Create, list, and delete file shares with quota management
Upload and download files with support for streaming large files
Create and manage nested directory structures
Create and access share snapshots for backup scenarios
Support for both connection string and Entra ID authentication
Async client support for high-throughput scenarios
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is a documentation guide for Azure Storage File Share SDK for Python. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 risk patterns detected. The skill uses standard Azure SDK patterns with proper credential handling via environment variables and DefaultAzureCredential. No malicious patterns, command injection risks, or prompt injection attempts were found.
valueStatement: Working with Azure Storage file shares requires understanding complex SDK patterns and authentication methods. This skill provides ready-to-use code examples for all common file share operations.
userTitle: Manage Azure File Shares with Python SDK
limitations: Requires valid Azure Storage account and credentials
Network connectivity to Azure Storage endpoints required
Does not support on-premises file share scenarios without Azure hybrid configuration
Rate limits and quotas depend on Azure Storage account tier

useCases.title: Cloud-Native Application Storage
useCases.description: Build applications that store and retrieve files from Azure file shares with full SDK support for upload, download, and management operations.

useCases.title: Legacy Application Migration
useCases.description: Migrate on-premises SMB file share workloads to Azure by implementing cloud-compatible file operations using the Python SDK patterns.

useCases.title: Automated Backup Workflows
useCases.description: Create automated backup solutions using share snapshots and programmatic file management for data protection scenarios.

promptTemplates.title: Basic File Upload
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to upload a text file to an Azure file share using connection string authentication.

promptTemplates.title: List Shares and Files
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to list all file shares in my storage account and then list the files in a specific share directory.

promptTemplates.title: Entra ID Authentication Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me configure Azure file share access using Entra ID authentication with DefaultAzureCredential for production use.

promptTemplates.title: Large File Streaming
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to download a large file from Azure file share without loading it entirely into memory. Show me the streaming approach with chunk processing.

bestPractices: Use environment variables for connection strings and account URLs to keep credentials out of source code
Prefer Entra ID authentication with DefaultAzureCredential for production workloads over connection strings
Stream large files using chunked downloads to avoid memory issues with files exceeding available RAM
antiPatterns: Hardcoding connection strings or account keys directly in source code
Loading entire large files into memory instead of using streaming downloads
Failing to close async clients explicitly which can cause resource leaks

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure Storage for file share operations?
faq.answer: Use either connection strings (simplest for development) or Entra ID with DefaultAzureCredential (recommended for production). Connection strings are set via AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING environment variable, while Entra ID uses AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_URL.

faq.question: What is the maximum file size I can upload to Azure file shares?
faq.answer: Azure file shares support files up to 100 TiB in premium accounts and 1 TiB in standard accounts. For large files, use streaming upload with range operations to upload in chunks.

faq.question: How do I create a point-in-time backup of a file share?
faq.answer: Use the create_snapshot() method on a ShareClient to create a read-only snapshot. You can then access the snapshot using get_share_client() with the snapshot parameter set to the snapshot timestamp.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Azure Storage Emulator or Azurite?
faq.answer: Yes, use the emulator connection string format (UseDevelopmentStorage=true) for Azurite or the legacy emulator. This allows local development without an Azure subscription.

faq.question: How do I handle errors when file operations fail?
faq.answer: The Azure SDK raises exceptions from azure.core.exceptions module. Wrap operations in try-except blocks and handle specific exceptions like ResourceNotFoundError, ResourceExistsError, and HttpResponseError.

faq.question: Is async support available for all file share operations?
faq.answer: Yes, the azure.storage.fileshare.aio module provides async versions of all clients. Use async patterns for high-throughput scenarios and remember to close async clients explicitly using await close().
